verses.text: These are the wise sayings of Solomon. Solomon was the son of David, and Solomon was king of Israel.
Proverbs will help you learn to be wise, to accept correction, and to understand wise sayings.
Proverbs will teach you to develop your mind in the right way. You will learn to do what is right and to be honest and fair
Proverbs teach people who know very little to be wise. They will teach young people what they need to know and how to use what they have learned
Even the wise can become wiser by listening to these proverbs. They will gain understanding from others to learn how to solve difficult problems.
Better to be poor and honest than to be a liar and a fool.
I is not good for a person to be without knowledge. If you hurry and do something too quickly, you will make mistakes.
People destroy their lives when they do foolish things, then they get angry and blame the Lord and say it is his fault.
When someone is rich, they have a lot of friends, but if he becomes poor, he has no friends.
A false witness will be punished, and someone who tells lies will not escape.
Everyone wants to be friends with an important person. They also want to be friends with someone who gives away gifts. Provers
A poor man’s brothers do not like him, and his friends will avoid him even more. When he really needs them, they stay away from him. there are no one there to help him.
To obtain wisdom is to love yourself; those who do what they have learn shall do good
A false witness will be punished, and someone who tells lies will not escape.
It is not normal for a fool to be rich or for a slave to rule over princes!
A wise man does not become angry quickly. He always wants to forgive the mistakes of other people.
The shouts of an angry king are like a roaring lion, but his kind words are like the dew on the grass.
A foolish child brings a lot of trouble to his father and a complaining wife is like the constant dripping of water.
14 People receive houses and money from their parents, but a good wise wife is a gift from the LORD.
A lazy people wants to sleep all day, and someone who will not work will not have any food to eat.
People who obey the commandments of the LORD will live. But he that ignores God’s ways shall die.
Whoever is kind to the poor lends to the LORD, and he will reward them for what they have done.
Discipline your children while there is still hope. Avoiding it because of their crying will end badly for them.
A person who gets angry quickly will always have trouble. If you help him, you will have to help him again and again.
If you listen to what people teach you and you learn from them, you will have wisdom in the end.
People make a lot of plans about what they want to do, but the advice of the LORD will endure.
The most important thing that you want from other people is for them to kind. It is better to be poor than to be a liar.
The fear the LORD and you will have a good life, one that is satisfying and protected during trouble.
A lazy man puts his hand in the bowl to take food, but he is too lazy to bring back his hand to his mouth.
If you punish a mocker, the simpleminded will learn a lesson; if you correct the wise, they will be all the wiser.
Children who would steal from their father and chase away their mother are a child that causes shame and bring disappointment.
My son, if you stop learning, you will turn your back on knowledge.
Using a criminal as a witness makes a joke of justice. Sinful people love to do things that are wrong.
Judgments are prepared for mockers, and stripes for the back of fools.”
Wine is deceiving, and alochal causes fighting; everyone led astray by them lacks wisdom.
People should be afraid of an angry king, like they are of a lion that roars. If you make the king angry, you can lose your life.
It is a honor for people to stop from strife: Any fool can start an argument.
Because of the cold a lazy person will not prepare their fields: then in the summer they shall beg for food, and no one shall given to them.
Getting information from someone can be like getting water from a deep well. If you are wise, you can draw it out.
There are many people who say: ‘You can trust me.’ But there are only a few of them that do what they promise.
Good people live in the right way and are honest. God blesses their children who live like they do.
When a king sits on his throne to judge people, he separates out all evil with his eyes.
No one can say: Who can say, “I have made my heart clean, have never done anything sinful.’
The LORD hates people that use the wrong weights and measures to cheat others.
Even children show what they are like by the things they do. You can see if their actions are pure and right.
The Lord has given some people ears to hear and some people eyes to see.
Do not love excessive sleep, or you will become poor; Open your eyes so that you can do your work and you will be satisfied with bread.
When a person buying something, they always say, “It’s no good. It costs too much.” Then they go away and tell others what a good deal they got.
There is gold, and an abundance of jewels; But the words of someone who is wise are even more precious.
Take the garment of anyone who puts up collateral for a stranger; hold it in pledge if he does it for an unfamiliar woman.
If you have cheated someone to get food, it will taste good in the beginning but in the end, The guilt and consequence will be bitter.
Get good advice before you make your plans. Before you start a war, ask for the advice of wise advisors.
A gossip goes around telling secrets. Don’t be friends with someone who does not care what he says.
Those who would curse their father or mother are like a lamp that goes out in the darkest night.
If someone gets money that he inherited too quickly, when he is too young, It will not be a blessing in the end.
Don’t say, “I will get even for this wrong.” Wait for the LORD to handle the matter.
The Lord hates people who cheat when they weigh things and who are dishonest
No person knows what will happen to him. It is the Lord who decides what will happen to us.
Think carefully before you promise to give something to God. Later, you might wish you had not made that promise.
A king who is wise can find out who is guilty and punish those who have done wrong.
The LORD gave us our mind and conscience; we cannot hide our thoughts and doings from Him.
If a king does what he has promised and he is faithful to his people, but they must be merciful for their kingdoms to last.
We admire a young man for his strength, but we respect an old man for his gray hair.
Sometimes it takes a painful experience to make us change our ways.
These are the wise sayings that King Lemuel’s mother taught him:
My child, I am your mother. I prayed to God and He gave you to me.
Don’t waste your strength or your life on women. Not give your efforts on things that destroy kings.
Lemuel, kings should not drink wine, Or for rulers to desire strong drink.
If they drink too much, they will not remember the laws and will not be able to judge the people in trouble rightly.
Alcohol is for the dying, and wine for those of bitter of heart
Let them drink and forget their troubles. Let them forget about all their suffering.
Speak up for people who cannot speak for themselves. Help people who are in trouble.
Speak for them and be fair when you judge, and protect the people who can not help themselves and who have nothing.
A wife of honest character is hard to find? She is worth far more than rubies.
Her husband trust her. He will never have to steal because he is poor
She does good for her husband all her life, and she never brings him trouble.
She gets wool and flax and enjoys making things with her hands.
She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.
She gets up while it is dark and she prepares food for her family and for the servants who work for her.

She looks carefully at a field and buys it and she plants the vineyard Proverbs 31: 17 Her arms are strong, and she keeps on working. Proverbs 31: 18 When she sees people wanting to buy the things that she makes, she works all night.
She makes thread with her hands, and she weaves at the loom and makes clothes.
She loves giving to poor people and those who can not protect themselves and who have nothing.
She does not worry about her family when it snows because they all have warm clothes.
She makes blankets and her clothes are made of linen and purple wool.
Everyone knows her husband. He sits with the leaders of the land at the gate.
She makes clothes and sells them, and she gives the merchants sashes to sell.
She is a strong and a neat woman, and people honour her. She is not worried about what will happen.
She always speaks words of wisdom, and she teaches her people to be friendly.
She oversees the care of her house. She is never lazy. Proverbs 31: 28Her children respect and praise her, and her husband says she is a good wife. He says:
‘There are a lot of good women, but you are better than all of them.’
A woman does not always stay young and beautiful, but you should always praise a woman who serves and fears the Lord.
She must get the reward for her hard work, and everyone in the city must praise her.
